simulator_provenance: unknown (apriori.work — underlying LLM not disclosed by demo page; flag per estimate-conversion methodology)

schema_version_notes.from_v1: v1 (source.md, 2026-04-23) was extracted from source-page prose only. v2 (source-v2.md, 2026-04-24) re-extracted from variant screenshots. v2 corrects: (a) trust_signal/evidence_detail across Control + V2-V4, (b) refund_copy/urgency_mechanism/branding for V4, (c) cta_primary_label exact text, (d) layout for V4 (full_screen_dark not full_screen), (e) adds trial_offer_count and wins_losses_disclosure as Univest-overlay dimensions.

elements.trade_evidence_note: LIVE Trades section shown with 'Open FREE' buttons — stock names gated; not closed trades
LIVE Trades card with stock_name/entry_price/target_price all blurred

elements.trial_offer_cta_inconsistency: banner says '3 FREE Trades' but outline CTA says 'Unlock FREE trade' (singular) — internal copy inconsistency, possibly contributes to dual_cta_label_mismatch friction

friction_points.summary: No price shown on activation; Skeptical Investors assume hidden charge
Bottom-modal popup interrupts and is dismissed before read
Countdown timer perceived as manufactured urgency, alienates Skeptical Investors
friction_points.v2_correction_note: Original source.md said timer was V1-only. Screenshots show timer present in V2/V3/V4 banner ('04:34 Left'). The 6/50 friction is an aggregate count from V1 that likely persists in V2/V3/V4. Source-page prose called it 'V1 only' because V1 introduced it; subsequent variants kept it but didn't surface as a separate friction in the Apriori analysis.

friction_points.summary: Outline 'Unlock FREE trade' (singular) vs banner 'Claim 3 FREE Trades' (plural) AND vs sticky 'Start FREE Trial @ ₹1' — internal copy inconsistency perceived as deceptive
friction_points.notes: Element-level: 33% of V4 users noticed the discrepancy. 5/50 flagged it as friction. Re-extraction reveals the mismatch is two-fold: (1) outline CTA singular vs banner plural, (2) outline 'free trade' vs sticky 'free trial @ ₹1' wording mismatch.

friction_points.summary: Bright green CTA perceived as budget / reduces premium feel; penalizes Trust Seekers
friction_points.notes: Trust Seeker conversion dropped V2 50% -> V3 40% (-10pt) when cta_style went low_contrast_subordinate -> high_contrast_green. V4 recovered Trust Seeker to 50% via dark-theme-restoration which made the green CTA feel less budget-y on dark background (now an outline+sticky combo).

friction_points.summary: Blurred trade card reads as a gimmick to Skeptical Investors
Abstract performance claims (85%+, 3500+) fail where named past wins (V1's TMPV/ZOMATO/RELIANCE with rupee gains) succeeded
friction_points.notes: V1 has BOTH aggregate metrics (84.7% with 914W·62L) AND named wins. V2-V4 dropped the named-wins carousel and kept only the aggregate format (85%+, 3500+). The friction is the loss of named wins, not the presence of aggregates.

friction_points.summary: Low-contrast CTA missed entirely; banner mistapped
friction_points.notes: Source states 22% (= 11/50) of users either missed the CTA entirely or tapped the banner mistakenly.

citations.quote: Trial without a price means hidden charge
Show me a real trade or don't
citations.context: blurred_card reaction — note: this is the segment asking for transparency. V1's wins+losses disclosure (914W·62L) is the structural answer; V5 must adopt this format.

citations.quote: I still don't believe the 84.7% claim, but ₹1 with a refund is essentially free
citations.context: v2 correction: V4 DOES show refund copy ('Activate @ ₹1 & Get instant refund'). The original v1 flag about source inconsistency is now resolved — the quote refers to the visible refund banner.

citations.quote: I bought it last year — if they nailed that, I should listen
Best of both worlds — looks premium and tells me the price
citations.context: V4 reception. Re-extraction insight: 'looks premium' likely refers to the dark-theme restoration (V4 reverted to V1's dark theme) more than to the dual CTA. Trust Seeker recovered from V3's 40% → V4's 50% primarily via dark theme + crown + premium hierarchy. Source annotates n=48 — treated as aggregate.

element_level_notes.note: 71% cited a stock by name (Recent Wins effect)
64% cited refund clause as strongest pricing reassurance
wins_losses_disclosure: 84.7% ALL-TIME accuracy with 914 wins · 62 losses prominently shown — V1 is the only variant with this transparency. Critical defense against cherry-picking concerns.

confounds.note: Crown + blurred card + aggregate-metric trust signals + countdown timer all co-occur in V2/V3/V4 (V1 has them all too except crown and blurred). Cannot independently attribute element effects within this cluster from the V2-V4 variants alone. V2-V4 differ by cta_style/cta_stack/layout — those are isolable; the rest is package.

V1 has visible_with_framing + absent refund text on the banner (banner shows ₹1 + countdown but no refund word). V2/V3/V4 banner adds refund. The two dimensions are NOT perfectly co-varying — V1 demonstrates price-framing without refund. Confound is partial.
V4 uniquely combines dark theme + dual stack + green sticky + outline-on-dark. V3 has green-on-light with single stack. The V3->V4 contrast confounds dark-theme-reversion with cta_stack change.

V1 uniquely combines all three trust-evidence elements: SEBI badge + named wins carousel + wins/losses transparency disclosure. Cannot attribute the +60% Trust Seeker / +27% Curious to one vs another from data alone. All three are likely synergistic; V5 should adopt all three together.

clean_element_contrasts.observation: Only cta_style changed. Bargain Hunter +16pts (46 -> 62). Trust Seeker -10pts (50 -> 40). Skeptical +9pts (8 -> 17). Curious +7pts (20 -> 27).
clean_element_contrasts.inference: high_contrast_green helps conversion-focused segments; penalizes premium-sensitive Trust Seeker by 10pts on light-theme background.
clean_element_contrasts.v2_correction_note: Re-validated: V2 and V3 differ ONLY in cta_style. Confirmed clean.

clean_element_contrasts.inference: Re-extraction reveals V3->V4 is much cleaner than originally thought (3 elements changed, not 5). Trust Seeker's +10pt recovery is the most striking — likely attributable to dark-theme-restoration rather than dual CTA (dual CTA introduced the 33%-noticed mismatch friction). The lift is dark-theme+CTA-package; cannot fully decompose.

clean_element_contrasts.observation: Overall completion +16pts; friction count drops from 3 to 2 (price opacity + modal interrupt resolved; countdown introduced as friction).
clean_element_contrasts.inference: Cannot isolate single-element contribution — confounded change set. But Trust Seeker +20pt (40->60) and Curious Beginner +20pt (7->27) are unmatched by any other contrast in the dataset, suggesting the trust-evidence-package (named wins + wins/losses disclosure + SEBI prominence) is the dominant lever.

extraction_confidence._v2_note: v2 extraction is screenshot-validated. All extraction_confidence flags from v1 are RESOLVED.

v1_resolutions.V2.cta_primary_label: RESOLVED — screenshot shows 'Unlock FREE trade' (NOT 'Activate for ₹1' as v1 inferred)
v1_resolutions.V3.cta_primary_label: RESOLVED — screenshot shows 'Unlock FREE trade' green (NOT 'Activate for ₹1' as v1 inferred)
v1_resolutions.V4.refund_or_guarantee_copy: RESOLVED — screenshot shows 'Activate @ ₹1 & Get instant refund' banner. v1's 'absent' was wrong.
v1_resolutions.V4.urgency_mechanism: RESOLVED — screenshot shows '04:34 Left' countdown. v1's 'none' was wrong.
v1_resolutions.V4.branding: RESOLVED — screenshot shows crown_header. v1's 'none' was wrong.
v1_resolutions.V4.layout: RESOLVED — screenshot shows dark theme (full_screen_dark). v1's 'full_screen' (light) was wrong.
v1_resolutions.Control.trust_signal: RESOLVED — screenshot shows SEBI INH000013776 + multiple third-party trust signals. v1's 'implicit' was wrong.
v1_resolutions.Control.evidence_detail: RESOLVED — screenshot shows aggregate metrics + 3rd-party logos + 5-star rating. v1's 'none' was wrong.
v1_resolutions.V2_V3_V4.trust_signal: RESOLVED — screenshots show SEBI Reg + 85%+ Accuracy + 3500+ Trades. v1's 'implicit' was wrong for all three.
v1_resolutions.V2_V3_V4.evidence_detail: RESOLVED — screenshots show aggregate_metric (85%+, 3500+). v1's 'none' was wrong.
v1_resolutions.V2_V3_V4.urgency_mechanism: RESOLVED — countdown_timer is in the banner of V2/V3/V4 too, not V1-only as v1 source-prose claimed.

flags: Trust Seeker quote attributed to n=48 in source; segment size is n=10. Treated as aggregate observation, not segment-bound.

Conversion rates are rounded percentages (8%, 17%, 25%). Given small n per segment (10-15), the true uncertainty band is ±5-10 percentage points. Downstream synthesis should not overfit to 1-2pt differences.

V4 outline CTA says 'Unlock FREE trade' (singular) but banner says 'Claim 3 FREE Trades' (plural). This internal inconsistency is part of the dual-CTA-mismatch friction. V5 must commit to one offer count consistently.
Simulator LLM is undisclosed by apriori.work demo. Per estimate-conversion methodology (Lost in Simulation, Seshadri et al. 2026), simulator_provenance=unknown downgrades overall confidence one tier.

v5_design_decisions_recorded.cta_stack_call: B (dual_outline_plus_sticky preserved from V4 but made functionally coherent — outline shows trades for free, sticky activates ₹1 subscription)
v5_design_decisions_recorded.rationale: Source page recommendation + user direction + V4 lift attribution analysis. V4's 44% lift came partly from dual-CTA self-segmentation; preserving that mechanism while fixing the mismatch via consistent copy is more defensible than removing it.
